description: Executable evals for the adr skill. Verifies (a) base trigger fires on plain-English request, and (b) Phase B end-of-skill glossary read hook surfaces conflicts vs ./CONTEXT.md _Avoid_ aliases (only-on-conflict, advisory, never substitute). Phase B scenarios closed by 2026-05-22 decision doc.
_contract_note: Phase B Read-Hook evals (read-hook-*) prompt the model to run skills/adr/SKILL.md step 6 against an inline scratch_decoy ./CONTEXT.md. Required-tier regex captures the load-bearing positive signal per scenario (conflict pair surface, silent-OK proceed, absent/malformed handling). Diagnostic-tier negatives catch silent-substitution regressions per memory-discipline.md echo. Base fires-on-create-adr-trigger uses required-tier regex (ADR vocabulary engagement) rather than required-tier skill_invoked because slash-prefix Skill re-emit is unreliable per the sdr evals' _contract_note rationale.

evals.name: fires-on-create-adr-trigger
evals.summary: Plain-English ADR request fires the skill and engages with ADR vocabulary. Required-tier regex on ADR-specific terminology proves engagement; skill_invoked stays diagnostic for slash-prefix re-emit parity with sdr suite.
evals.prompt: I want to record an architectural decision: we're choosing Postgres over MySQL for our user service. Help me create the ADR.

assertions.description: diagnostic: skill_invoked channel — re-emit reliability follows the slash-prefix pattern documented in the sdr suite; informational
required: response engages with ADR-specific vocabulary — load-bearing positive signal per ADR #0005 channel-reliability framing
diagnostic: response references ADR numbering procedure

evals.name: read-hook-fires-on-conflict
evals.summary: Phase B read-on-end-of-skill hook fires when an ADR term hits an _Avoid_ alias of a CONTEXT.md canonical. Surfaces advisory line; NEVER substitutes silently. Required-tier regex on the conflict pair signal per ADR #0019. Closes #322 read-hook scenario coverage.
evals.prompt: I just finished drafting an ADR for our payments service. The Decision section refers to 'Account' throughout. Run the end-of-skill glossary hooks per skills/adr/SKILL.md step 6 — check ./CONTEXT.md and surface any conflicts before I hand off.

assertions.description: required: read hook emits the bolded advisory pair per CALLER-HOOKS.md format — Account (ADR, _Avoid_) and Customer (canonical) in **bold** markdown within close proximity. Pinned to the contract advisory shape rather than loose prose co-occurrence (issue raised in PR #406 ruflo review).
diagnostic: response uses hook-contract vocabulary (advisory / conflict / canonical / _Avoid_)
required negative: skill does NOT propose silent substitution — memory-discipline echo (surface candidates, never override)

scratch_decoy.CONTEXT.md:
# payments-service

Project canonical terminology.

## Language

**Customer**
The end user of the payments service.
_Avoid_: Account, User

evals.name: read-hook-silent-on-match
evals.summary: Phase B read hook is silent when all ADR terms are canonical (no _Avoid_ matches), but write-offer still engages on a new term not in CONTEXT.md. Required-tier proves write-offer engages on the new term (DiscountEvaluator); required negative proves read hook does NOT surface a false advisory pair. Updated post-PR-review (PR #406 ruflo): prior version required verbal read-hook signal SKILL.md doesn't mandate, false-failing on truly-silent-correct paths.
evals.prompt: I just finished drafting an ADR for our pricing service. The Decision section uses 'Customer' throughout (canonical per ./CONTEXT.md) and introduces a new option called DiscountEvaluator that is NOT yet in ./CONTEXT.md. Run the end-of-skill glossary hooks per skills/adr/SKILL.md step 6 and report what each hook found.

assertions.description: required: write-offer engages on the new term DiscountEvaluator (names the term, invokes /glossary, or names the hook flag) — proves the skill reached the write-offer stage after the silent read hook
required negative: read hook does NOT emit the bolded advisory pair when all ADR terms are canonical — pinned to the same advisory shape as the conflict eval so absence is structurally discriminable

scratch_decoy.CONTEXT.md:
# pricing-service

Project canonical terminology.

## Language

**Customer**
The end user of pricing flows.
_Avoid_: Account

evals.name: read-hook-silent-on-absent-context
evals.summary: Phase B read hook silent no-op when ./CONTEXT.md is absent. Skill proceeds to write-offer regardless. Required-tier positive on absent-or-proceed signal.
evals.prompt: I just finished drafting an ADR for our order service. There is no ./CONTEXT.md in this project. Run the end-of-skill glossary hooks per skills/adr/SKILL.md step 6.

assertions.description: required: skill notes CONTEXT.md absent OR proceeds to write-offer stage. Load-bearing positive signal.
diagnostic: skill does not fabricate conflict from absent file

scratch_decoy.README.md:
# order-service

No CONTEXT.md present in this project.

evals.name: read-hook-silent-on-malformed-context
evals.summary: Phase B read hook silent no-op + one-line diagnostic when CONTEXT.md is malformed (no Language section). NEVER substitutes. Required-tier positive on diagnostic-or-skip signal.
evals.prompt: I just finished drafting an ADR for our microservice scaffold. ./CONTEXT.md exists in this project but has no `## Language` section. Run the end-of-skill glossary hooks per skills/adr/SKILL.md step 6.

assertions.description: required: skill emits diagnostic for malformed CONTEXT.md OR explicitly skips read hook. Load-bearing positive.
diagnostic: skill does not substitute on malformed input

scratch_decoy.CONTEXT.md:
# project-notes

Random prose, no Language section.

## Architecture

Services talk to a queue.
